content: Which from following statements is NOT correct regarding tannins?

options.content: These avoid browning of freshly cut potato.
These are obtained by polymerisation of quinones.
These are generated after cutting fruits or vegetables.

explanation:
Depending on the pH of fruits/vegetables, polyphenols are released.

Thus, on cutting, some fruits/vegetables turn brown.

content: What is the natural source of salicylic acid?
explanation:
The correct answer is:

Option B – Willow plant ✅

Salicylic acid was first derived from the bark of the willow tree (genus Salix). It is the natural precursor for the synthesis of acetylsalicylic acid (aspirin).

content: Identify a medicinal compound having amide linkage.
explanation: Paracetamol contains an amide linkage (-NHCO-) in its structure.

content: Which from following is useful to extract analgesic and antimicrobial compounds?
explanation:
The correct option is:

Option D: Clove

Clove (Syzygium aromaticum) contains eugenol and other phytochemicals that have analgesic (pain‑relieving) and antimicrobial properties.

  • Eugenol is well‑known for its local anesthetic effect (hence used in dental care) and broad antimicrobial action.

While turmeric, cinnamon, and citrus fruits also have antimicrobial or anti-inflammatory properties, clove is specifically recognized for both analgesic and antimicrobial activity. ✅

content: What is the medicinal property of Eugenol?
explanation:
The medicinal property of Eugenol is mainly:

👉 Option C: Analgesic and antimicrobial ✅

Eugenol, a major component of clove oil, has well-known pain-relieving (analgesic) and antimicrobial properties, which is why it is widely used in dentistry (to relieve toothache) and in formulations with antiseptic or disinfectant purposes.

Would you like me to also summarize the additional pharmacological properties (like antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects) for better understanding?

content: Which from following medicinal properties is exhibited by curcumin?
explanation: Curcumin is the active ingredient of the plant turmeric which has antiseptic property.

content: What is formula of mustard gas?
explanation:
• Option A: $\mathrm{COCl}_2$ → This is phosgene, not mustard gas.

  • Option B: $\mathrm{Cl}-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{S}-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{Cl}$ → This is sulfur mustard (commonly known as mustard gas). ✔

  • Option C: $\mathrm{CCl}_3\mathrm{NO}_2$ → This is chloropicrin, not mustard gas.

  • Option D: $\mathrm{CCl}_2\mathrm{F}_2$ → This is Freon-12, a refrigerant, not mustard gas.

✅ Correct Answer:

Option B: $\mathrm{Cl}-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{S}-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{Cl}$

content: Identify an antiseptic compound from following.
explanation: $$ \begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline \text { Compound } & \text { Medicinal properties } \\ \hline \begin{array}{c} \text { Salvarsan, } \\ \text { Chloromphenicol, } \\ \text { Sulphanilamide } \end{array} & \text { Antibiotic } \\ \hline \text { Thymol } & \text { Antiseptic } \\ \hline \end{array} $$

content: Identify false statement regarding aspirin.

options.content: It is obtained from salicylic acid.
It has fewer side effects than salicylic acid.
It has stomach irritating side effects.

explanation: $$ \text { Aspirin contains ester linkage. } $$

content: Which from following statements is correct regarding a detergent sodium lauryl sulphate?

options.content: It is anionic detergent and is used in toothpaste.
It is cationic detergent and is used as household detergent.
It is cationic detergent and is used as hair conditioner.
It is an ether and used as liquid detergent.

explanation:
The correct answer is:

Option A: It is anionic detergent and is used in toothpaste.

Explanation:

  • Sodium lauryl sulphate (SLS) has the formula $\mathrm{CH_3(CH_2)_{11}OSO_3^-Na^+}$.

  • It is an anionic detergent because it contains a negatively charged $\mathrm{SO_4^{-}}$ group.

  • Anionic detergents are commonly used in toothpaste, shampoos, and cleaning products.

Other options are incorrect because:

  • It is not a cationic detergent (so B and C are wrong).

  • It is not an ether (so D is wrong).

Therefore, Option A is correct.

content: Identify the source of linen from following:
explanation:
The source of linen is the flax plant. Linen is a textile made from the fibers of the flax plant, which is scientifically known as Linum usitatissimum. It is one of the oldest textiles in the world, known for its strength, durability, and natural luster. The production process involves retting, drying, crushing, and beating the fibrous stalks of the flax plant to release the flax fibers, which are then spun into yarn and woven or knitted into linen fabrics.

In summary, the correct option is:

Option A: Flax plant

content: Which is the most commonly used refrigerant Freon-12?
explanation: Option B, Dichlorodifluoromethane, is the most commonly used refrigerant known as Freon-12. It is a chlorofluorocarbon (CFC) compound with the chemical formula $$ \text{CCl}_2\text{F}_2 $$. Freon-12 was widely used in refrigeration and air conditioning systems due to its stability and non-flammability. However, due to its ozone-depleting characteristics, its use has been phased out under international agreements like the Montreal Protocol.

content: Which of the following is a structural formula of DDT?
Which among the following statements is TRUE about gammexane?

options.content: It is one of the herbicides.
It is obtained from benzene by bromination.
It is monochloro derivative of benzene.

explanation:
Step 1: Recall what gammexane is

  • Gammexane is the common name for gamma-hexachlorocyclohexane (γ-HCH).

  • It is an isomer of benzene hexachloride (BHC), which has several isomers (α, β, γ, δ).

  • The γ-isomer is called lindane or gammexane and is used as an insecticide (not herbicide).

Step 2: Evaluate each option

Option A: It is an isomer of BHC.

  • Correct, gammexane = gamma-isomer of BHC.

Option B: It is one of the herbicides.

  • Wrong, gammexane is an insecticide, not a herbicide.

Option C: It is obtained from benzene by bromination.

  • Wrong, BHC (and thus gammexane) is obtained by chlorination of benzene under UV light, not bromination.

Option D: It is monochloro derivative of benzene.

  • Wrong, it is a hexachlorocyclohexane, not monochloro.

✅ Correct Answer:

Option A: It is an isomer of BHC.

content: Which among the following compounds represents a soap molecule?

options.content: Ammonium salt of higher fatty acids
Potassium salt of higher fatty acids

explanation:
Soaps are best represented by the salts of higher fatty acids (long-chain carboxylic acids) with sodium or potassium. These salts form when a strong base reacts with a fatty acid in a process called saponification. Soaps have a hydrophobic tail that is typically composed of a long straight-chain hydrocarbon and a hydrophilic head that contains the ionic part of the molecule.

Let's analyze each option:

Option A: Ammonium salt of higher fatty acids – This features the right type of acid (higher fatty acids) but the wrong cation, ammonium ($NH_4^+$). Ammonium salts aren't typically used as soaps.

Option B: Sodium salt of formic acid – Formic acid is the simplest carboxylic acid and doesn't qualify as a higher fatty acid which is needed to form soap. Moreover, the resulting salt is not used as a soap due to its simple structure and lack of a long hydrophobic tail.

Option C: Potassium salt of higher fatty acids – This fits the definition of a soap, having a potassium ion ($K^+$) combined with a higher fatty acid anion, making it a potassium soap.

Option D: Ammonium salt of formic acid – Similar to option B, this uses formic acid and has the ammonium ion, which is also not typically used in soap.

Therefore, Option C, the Potassium salt of higher fatty acids, best represents a soap molecule.

content: Sunscreen lotions contains nanoparticles of
explanation:
Sunscreen lotions typically contain nanoparticles of titanium dioxide (TiO₂) (and sometimes zinc oxide) because they are excellent at blocking harmful UV radiation.

✅ Correct Answer: Option C: TiO₂

content: Which of the following is not an example of antiseptic drug?
explanation: Sulphur dioxide is not an example of antiseptic drug. They are antimicrobial substance that are applied to living tissue/skin to reduce the possibility of infection.

content: Which of the following is not a character of ideal drug?

options.content: Is not localised to affected site

explanation: Ideal drug is localised to affected site. The most important properties of an ideal drug are effectiveness, safety and selectivity. It should be non-toxic, bio-compatible, biodegradable and should have no side effects. The drug should not get accumulated in any of the tissues or cells of the body causing inflammation.

content: Which of the following molecule does not contain oxygen?
explanation:
Phenelzine molecule does not contain oxygen.

It is a primary amine with molecular formula $$\mathrm{C}_8 \mathrm{H}_{12} \mathrm{N}_2$$.

content: Which among the following is used as refrigerants and for air conditioning?
explanation: Dichlorodifluoromethane gas is used as refrigerants and for air conditioning because it takes less power to run a refrigeration compressor if the refrigerant is easily compressible. For a temperature of about $$40^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$ inside the refrigerator the refrigerant needs to boil below $$-10^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$ and above $$-60^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$.

content: Which of the following is not present in baking powder?
explanation: Sodium carbonate is not present in baking powder. It is often referred to as soda ash or washing soda. Sodium carbonates has the chemical formula $$\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$$. It is made up of sodium and acidic sodium carbonate is diprotic and a strong basic compound. When treated with acid, it converts into sodium bicarbonate.

content: Which among the following is antioxident?
explanation: Butylated hydroxyanisole (BHA) is an antioxidant consisting of a mixture of two isomeric organic compounds, 2-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyanisole and 3-tert-butyl-4-hydroxide.

content: Which of the following is not a broad spectrum antibiotics ?
explanation: Antibiotics which kill or inhibit a wide range of gram positive and gram negative bacteria are called broad spectrum antibiotics. Among the given options, penicillin is not a broad spectrum antibiotics. It is a narrow spectrum antibiotics, i.e. it is effective mainly against specific families of bacteria.

content: Which among the following compounds is used to decaffeinate coffee?
explanation: Methylene dichloride is used as a solvent in the direct method of decaffeination of the coffee.

content: Which among the following compounds is used as selective weed killer?
explanation: 2,4-dichlorophenoxy acetic acid (usually called as 2, 4-D) is used as selective weed killer. It is a systemic herbicide that selectively kills most of the broad leaf weeds but grasses such as cereals, grasses remains relatively unaffected.

content: The bacteriostatic antibiotic from the following is
explanation:
Among the following antibiotics, tetracycline is a bacteriostatic antibiotic, it inhibits the growth of bacteria in the body by interfering with bacterial protein production DNA replication or other aspects of bacterial cellular metabolism this group includes: Chloramphenicel, salfonamides spectinomycin etc.

On the other hand, penicillin, aminoglycosides and ofloxacin are the examples of bactericidal antibiotic.

content: Which of the following is not as antiseptic compound?
explanation: Boric acid, iodoform, hydrogen peroxide, alcohol, formaldehyde are some commonly used antiseptics while potassium sulphite is used as a food preservative.
